31^-41 ^ T Rerfr ^TT m ^ * K + K # R-iich 16 4 i ^ 0 , 2015^3Tf^r^TTW^TT^r.3TT. 534 (sr) % ?r^r ^ f r t e f w r n^rr «TT I MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 6th November, 2015 S.O. 3016 (E).—Whereas, the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967 (37 of 1967) (hereinafter referred to as the said Act) has been enacted to provide for more effective prevention of certain unlawful activities of individuals and associations and for dealing with terrorist activities and for matters connected therewith: -- 2 of 3 -- [w\ i i - ^ i s ;>(ii)] TRCT ^n uw^ •. sm^wn And whereas, clause (m) of sub-section (!) of section 2 of the said Act defines the terrorist nrganizafion to mean an organ i.sat ion listed in the First Schedule or an organisation operating under (he same name as an organisation so listed; And whereas, the First Schedule to the said Act specifies the list of terrorist organisations; And whereas the National Socialist Council of Nagaland (Khaplang) [NSCN(K)] , a militant outfit which came into existence in I 9SS is active along the Indo-Myanmar border. Dimapur. Mon, Longleng. Phek. Kiphire. Zunheboto and Knhima districts of Nagaland, Changlang, Longding and Tirap districts of Anmacha! Pradesh and parts of Tamenglong and Senapati districts of Manipnr and has been consolidating its position both organisationally and in terms of weapons and cadies to achieve the objective of a separate 'Sovereign Nagaland State', and has resorted to terrorism as a means to this end by killing innocent civilians and security forces and engaging in other violent activities including robbery, extortion, kidnapping and indi.scrimini.ite planting hind-mines; And whereas, the recent violent and terrorist activities of the NSCN(K) include, inter-alia. the following. namely;- (i) killing one Security Force personnel and two porters and also critically injuring nine Security Force personnel of 14th Assam Rifles at Indo-Myanmar border on 6lli February. 2015. falling under Jairam Sub- division of Changlang district. Arunachal Pradesh; (ii) injuring four Assam Rifles personnel on the 26tli March, 2015 by indiscriminately firing upon 19 personnel of Assam Rifles near Indira Gandhi Stadium in district Kohima, Nagaland; (iii) injuring four civilians by exploding a bomb at PWD Colony, Wokha Town, district Wokha, Nagaland, on the 26th March, 2015; (iv) killing seven and injuring nine Security Force personnel of Assam Rifles, killing one Security Force personnel of Naga Territorial Army in an ambush on the 3rd May, 2015, at village Changiangsiiii. under Tobu Police Station of district Mon, Nagaland; (v) killing 18 and injuring 15 Army personnel of 6 Dogra Regiment on 4th June. 2015 in Chandel District. Manipur in its attack on the Army convoy by using lethal weapons and rocket propelled grenades; And whereas, the Central Government believes that the National Socialist Council of Nagaland (Khaplang) [NSCN(K)] is involved in terrorism. Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by clause (a) of sub-section (I) of section 35 of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967, the Centra! Government hereby add the National Socialist Council of Nagaland (Khaplang) [NSCN(K)] and all its formation and front organisations as a terrorist organisation in the First Schedule to the said Act and for the said purpose makes the following amendments, namely: - In the First Schedule to the said Act, after serial number 38 and the entries relating thereto, the following serial number and entries shall be inserted, namely: - "39. National Socialist Council of Nagaland (Khaplang) FNSCN(K)], ail its formations and front organisations." [ F. No. IK111/45/20I5-NE-V] M.A. GANAPATHY. Jt. Secy. Note : The First Schedule to the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967 was last amended vide notification of the Government of India's number S.O. 534(E), dated the 16th February, 2015.
